title: VDD confabulation: ConfabulationTracker treats zero findings as 100 percent FP — terminates loop prematurely
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness. Location: src/vdd/confabulation.rs:37-46 (record_iteration). Violation: When genuine=0 and false_positives=0 (zero findings in an iteration), the code sets rate=1.0 with comment 'consider it a clean pass (FP rate 1.0 for convergence)'. But 1.0 >= threshold (default 0.75) means should_terminate() returns true — so a single zero-finding iteration at min_iterations terminates the loop declaring confabulation convergence. This conflates 'no findings' (good) with 'all findings were hallucinated' (bad), and the mod.rs:518-525 fallback path for zero-genuine-past-min is reachable only in advisory mode because record_iteration already poisons the tracker. Mandated Refactor: Differentiate NO_FINDINGS from 100 percent FP. Either (a) return Option<f32> and let should_terminate ignore None iterations, or (b) use a separate 'clean pass counter' that counts consecutive zero-finding iterations, terminating on count>=2. The current design guarantees a false 'converged by confabulation threshold' message whenever the adversary finds nothing.
